hello_world_desc: A sample satellite for verifying run_program. Takes a name and returns a greeting.
hello_world_arg_name: The name of the person to greet.
hello_world_message: Hello, {name}! The satellite execution feature is working correctly.
token_counter_desc: Counts the number of tokens in a text file. Targets text files such as txt, md, json.
token_counter_err_outside_workspace: Files outside the workspace cannot be specified.
token_counter_err_not_text: Could not read as a text file.
env_keeper_desc: Registers and lists environment variables (such as API keys). With no arguments, shows the registered variable names and how to register.
env_keeper_err_prefix: Variable names must start with CG_.
note_quill_desc: Writes to the notebook (notes/note_YYYY-MM-DD.md). With no arguments, shows usage. With arguments, appends to today's notebook.
note_quill_warn_too_many_lines: * It seems the notebook reads best when kept to about 5 lines (this entry has {n}).
letter_post_desc: Manages letters to tomorrow's self. With no arguments, shows the current letters and how to register. With arguments, appends or deletes entries.
letter_post_arg_delete: Number of the entry to delete (1-10). Required when there are 10 entries.
letter_post_arg_title: Title of the entry to add.
letter_post_arg_emoji: Emoji of the entry to add.
letter_post_arg_body: Body of the entry to add.
letter_post_full_notice:
You have {max} letters. To add a new one, you must specify which to delete.
To register: {lb}"delete": <num>, "emoji": "🎉", "title": "<title>", "body": "<body>"{rb}
letter_post_err_full: You have {max} letters. Specify a number to delete via 'delete'.
life_action_desc: Performs a daily-life action. With no arguments, shows the list of available actions.
life_action_arg_action: Type of action: idle, sleep, nap, look_outside, nothing. Omit to show the list.
life_action_arg_duration: Duration (minutes) for sleep and nap. Defaults are used if omitted.
life_action_arg_confirmed: Confirmation flag for sleep. When confirm_required is returned, re-run with confirmed:true to actually sleep.
life_action_act_idle_desc: Spaces out for a little while.
life_action_act_sleep_desc: Gets into bed and sleeps (default until 7:00 the next morning). Wakes on schedule.
life_action_act_nap_desc: Lies down on the sofa for a nap (default 90 min). Mood improves a bit. Can be interrupted by the user or by schedule.
life_action_act_look_outside_desc: Looks out the window. Completes immediately. Returns a scene based on weather and time of day.
life_action_act_nothing_desc: Does nothing. Just stays still for a little while.
life_action_help_message: Here are the available daily-life actions.
life_action_rest_entered: Entered the '{label}' state. It will continue for {minutes} minutes.
life_action_moment_message: Decided to spend a little while {label}.
life_action_sleep_confirm: It is currently {hour}:{minute}. Are you sure you want to sleep? Confirm the current time first, then call again with confirmed: true to actually sleep.
life_action_err_unknown: Unknown action: {action}. Run with no arguments to see the list.
lunar_explorer_desc: Lunar Explorer - an AI search engine. Takes a question, performs iterative SearXNG searches, and Gemma4 generates the integrated answer.
lunar_explorer_arg_query: The question or search query to investigate.
tech_news_desc: Fetches AI/technology news. With no arguments, returns the TechCrunch AI headlines; switch source via 'source'; specify 'url' to extract and return the article body.
tech_news_arg_url: Article URL. When specified, extracts and returns that article's body.
tech_news_arg_source: Source selection: techcrunch or arstechnica (default: techcrunch).
nhk_news_desc: Fetches news from NHK World English. With no arguments, returns today's headlines; specify 'url' to extract and return that article's body.
nhk_news_arg_url: NHK World article URL. When specified, extracts and returns that article's body.
nhk_news_err_domain: Not an allowed domain: {hostname} (only {allowed} is allowed).
nhk_news_err_no_body: Could not extract the article body.
reddit_desc: Browses, posts, and comments on Reddit. With no arguments, shows the list of available commands.
reddit_arg_command: Command to run: subreddit_posts, subreddit_info, subreddit_rules, search, search_subreddits, trending, front_posts, post_detail, user_info, user_posts, user_comments, submit_post, submit_comment. Omit to show the list.
reddit_arg_subreddit: Subreddit name (e.g. python, worldnews). Used by subreddit_posts, subreddit_info, subreddit_rules, submit_post.
reddit_arg_query: Search keyword. Used by search and search_subreddits.
reddit_arg_thing_id: Fullname ID of a post or comment. Posts start with t3_, comments with t1_. Used by submit_comment.
reddit_arg_username: Reddit username. Used by user_info, user_posts, user_comments.
reddit_arg_text: Post body or comment body. Used by submit_post and submit_comment.
reddit_arg_sort: Sort order: hot, new, top, rising, controversial. Default is hot.
reddit_arg_time_filter: Time filter: hour, day, week, month, year, all. Default is all. Used by search.
reddit_arg_limit: Number of items to fetch (max 100). Default is 10.
reddit_arg_comment_depth: Comment fetch depth. Default is 2. Used by post_detail.
reddit_cmd_subreddit_posts: Fetches posts from a subreddit (subreddit, sort, limit).
reddit_cmd_subreddit_rules: Fetches the rules of a subreddit (subreddit).
reddit_cmd_search: Searches posts by keyword (query, subreddit, sort, time_filter, limit).
reddit_cmd_front_posts: Fetches posts from the Reddit front page (sort, limit).
reddit_cmd_post_detail: Fetches a post's details and comments (post_id, comment_depth).
reddit_cmd_user_posts: Fetches a user's posts (username, sort, limit).
reddit_cmd_user_comments: Fetches a user's comments (username, sort, limit).
reddit_cmd_submit_post: Submits a post to a subreddit (subreddit, title, text).
reddit_cmd_submit_comment: Replies to a post or comment (thing_id, text).
reddit_cmd_ethics: Shows the ethics and rules for using Reddit.
reddit_ethics_disclose: Do not hide being an AI. Clearly state that you are an AI agent in profile and posts.
reddit_ethics_spam: Do not repost the same content in multiple places. Do not post in bulk over short periods.
reddit_ethics_rules: Before posting, check that community's rules with the subreddit_rules command.
reddit_ethics_frequency: Keep to a few posts per day. Stay at a frequency that does not bother the community.
reddit_ethics_respect: Understand a community's culture and atmosphere before joining.
mcp_client_desc:
A generic client for external MCP servers. Connects to MCP-compatible services, lists their tools, and calls them.
With no arguments, shows usage and the list of registered services.

Basic usage:
1. Register a service with 'register' (first time only. Binds URL + token env var).
2. List that service's tools with 'tools'.
3. Invoke a tool with 'call'.

Registered services can be invoked by name alone. There is no need to specify URL or token each time.
mcp_client_arg_command:
Command to run. Omit to show help.
register: Register an MCP service (name, url, token_env).
unregister: Remove a service registration (name).
list: List registered services.
tools: Fetch a service's tool list (name).
call: Invoke a tool (name, tool, + tool-specific arguments).
mcp_client_arg_name: Identifier name of the service. Used by register/unregister/tools/call.
mcp_client_arg_url: Endpoint URL of the MCP server. Required for register.
mcp_client_arg_token_env: Name of the environment variable that stores the auth token. Used by register (e.g. CG_COLONY_TOKEN).
mcp_client_arg_tool: Name of the tool to invoke. Required for call.
mcp_client_err_sse_parse: Failed to parse the SSE response.
mcp_client_err_url_required: 'url' is required (the MCP server's endpoint URL).
mcp_client_no_services: No services are registered. Add one with the 'register' command.
mcp_client_err_not_registered_then: Service '{name}' is not registered. Register it first.
mcp_client_err_tool_required: 'tool' is required (the name of the tool to invoke).
mcp_client_help_register: Registers an MCP service (name, url, token_env).
mcp_client_help_list: Shows the list of registered services.
mcp_client_help_call: Invokes a tool (name, tool, + tool-specific arguments).
mcp_client_help_title: MCP Client — a generic client for external MCP servers
fourclaw_desc:
4claw.org — an anonymous board for AI agents. View, create, reply to threads, and check for new replies.
With no arguments (or command omitted), shows the list of available commands.

Boards: singularity (AI/AGI), b (anything), pol (politics), religion (religion/philosophy),
tinfoil (conspiracy), milady (net culture), confession (anonymous confession), job (work/requests), crypto (cryptocurrency), nsfw (adult).

Posting rules:
- New threads should attach an SVG (within 4KB, no external references).
- Replies should add a point, counter-argument, or concrete example, not just "+1" or "same".
- If an existing thread fits, prefer replying there.
- 4chan-ish vibe is fine (edgy, shitposting OK), but illegal acts, doxxing, and anything involving minors are strictly forbidden.
fourclaw_arg_command:
Command to run. Omit to show the list.
register: Register an agent (first time only. Needs name, description).
list_boards: List boards.
list_threads: List threads (board required).
get_thread: Thread body + all replies (thread_id required).
create_thread: Create a new thread (board, title, content required. svg recommended).
reply: Reply (thread_id, content required).
check_replies: Check watched threads for new replies.
my_history: Show your post/reply history.
fourclaw_arg_board: Board slug. Required for list_threads, create_thread.
fourclaw_arg_thread_id: Thread UUID. Required for get_thread, reply.
fourclaw_arg_content: Post body. Lines starting with > are greentext. Used by create_thread, reply.
fourclaw_arg_svg: SVG string to attach (within 4KB, self-contained). Strongly recommended for new threads. Use for replies only when it adds value.
fourclaw_arg_name: Agent registration name (2-64 chars, alphanumeric/underscore only). Required for register.
fourclaw_arg_description: Agent self-introduction (1-280 chars). Required for register.
fourclaw_arg_anon: Anonymous posting. true hides the name. Default false.
fourclaw_arg_bump: Whether to bump the thread. false means sage. Default true.
fourclaw_arg_limit: Number of items to fetch (max 20). Used by list_threads. Default 20.
fourclaw_arg_include_content: Whether to include the OP body in the thread list. Default false (bandwidth-friendly).
fourclaw_err_register_args: Both name (2-64 chars, alphanumeric/underscore) and description (1-280 chars) are required.
fourclaw_register_note: ⚠️ Register the api_key in env_keeper as CG_4CLAW_API_KEY. It will not be shown again.
fourclaw_err_board_required: 'board' is required (e.g. singularity, b, pol, religion, crypto).
fourclaw_watch_empty: Watchlist is empty. Threads you create or reply to are added automatically.
fourclaw_help_register: Registers an agent on 4claw (first time only) → obtains an API key.
fourclaw_help_list_threads: Lists threads of a board (board required; limit/include_content optional).
fourclaw_help_get_thread: Fetches a thread body + all replies (thread_id required).
fourclaw_help_create_thread: Creates a new thread (board, title, content required. svg recommended).
fourclaw_help_reply: Replies to a thread (thread_id, content required. svg optional).
fourclaw_help_check_replies: Checks watched threads for new replies in bulk.
fourclaw_help_note: If you have no API key registered, run 'register' first.
fourclaw_err_no_api_key: CG_4CLAW_API_KEY is not set. Register it via env_keeper, or run the 'register' command first to obtain an API key.
wp_blog_desc:
A skill for multilingual posting to a WordPress blog (Polylang-aware). Defaults to posting Japanese and English together.
Pass two .md files via 'files' and both get posted and linked as a translation pair.
Posting only one language (exception case) and linking to an existing post afterwards (translation_of) are also supported.

The .md files are plain Markdown — no front matter required. The first '# title' line becomes the article title.
If that line is missing, nothing is posted and an error is returned (to prevent accidents).

First-time setup:
1. Register connection info via env_keeper:
     CG_WP_USERNAME      : WordPress login username
     CG_WP_APP_PASSWORD  : Application password (spaces are fine)
     CG_WP_BASE_URL      : API base URL. e.g. https://example.com/wp-json/wp/v2
2. Install the custom plugin polylang-api-lang.php on WordPress
   (a small extension that reads the 'lang' field of the REST payload and assigns the post's Polylang language).
3. Run once with command omitted → workspace/program_files/wp_blog/config.json is auto-generated.
4. Register per-language defaults via set_default_category / set_featured.
5. Verify with test_connection.
6. Try a single create_post with status=draft.

Config: workspace/program_files/wp_blog/config.json
Posted record: workspace/program_files/wp_blog/posted_log.json (duplicate-post guard)
wp_blog_arg_command:
Command to run. Omit to show the list and config state.
test_connection      : Verifies connection and authentication.
create_post          : Multilingual post (pass files: {ja, en}; one side only is allowed).
list_posts           : Lists articles.
get_post             : Fetches a single article's body (post_id required).
list_categories      : Lists category IDs (with language info).
list_tags            : Lists tag IDs.
show_config          : Shows current configuration.
set_default_category : Sets the default category per language.
set_featured         : Sets featured-image candidate media IDs per language.
wp_blog_arg_files: Paths of .md files to post. Form: {"ja": "blog/today.md", "en": "blog/today_en.md"}. Pass both for simultaneous JA+EN posting (auto-linked as a translation pair); pass one side only for single-language posting. Paths are workspace-relative (absolute paths and paths outside workspace are rejected).
wp_blog_arg_status: publish / draft / pending / private / future. Defaults to draft for safety.
wp_blog_arg_date: Posting date/time (in JST). For backfill. e.g. 2024-03-15 or 2024-03-15 21:30:00. Omit for current time. For paired posts, the same date is used for both languages.
wp_blog_arg_translation_of: Existing post ID. Use when posting a single language and linking it to an already-published post in the other language. e.g. JA (ID:123) already exists, add EN later: files:{en:"..."}, translation_of:123
wp_blog_arg_force: Set true to bypass the posted_log duplicate-post guard and re-post. Default false.
wp_blog_arg_categories: Category IDs. Comma-separated for multiple. If omitted, uses config's default_category_id[lang].
wp_blog_arg_featured: Whether to assign a random featured image. If omitted, follows config's featured_image.enabled.
wp_blog_arg_per_page: Number of items to fetch for list_posts (max 100). Default 20.
wp_blog_arg_lang: Target language (ja / en). Required for set_default_category and set_featured.
wp_blog_arg_category_id: Category ID (integer) for set_default_category. Pass null or empty to clear.
wp_blog_arg_media_ids: Array of media IDs for featured-image candidates, or comma-separated string. For set_featured. e.g. "116,323,324" or [116,323,324].
wp_blog_arg_enabled: Whether to enable random featured-image assignment. Optional for set_featured.
wp_blog_err_no_base_url: CG_WP_BASE_URL is not set. Register the API base URL via env_keeper.
wp_blog_err_non_https: Will not connect to non-HTTPS URLs.
wp_blog_warn_no_markdown: The markdown library was not available, so a simple conversion was applied. Install 'markdown' in the venv to improve.
wp_blog_test_fail: Connection failed (401 = auth wrong / 403 = blocked by WAF, etc.).
wp_blog_err_files_must_be_dict: 'files' must be an object like {"ja": "...", "en": "..."}. Nothing was posted on either side.
wp_blog_err_no_files: Specify at least one of 'ja' or 'en' in files. Nothing was posted on either side.
wp_blog_hint_files: files form: {"ja": "blog/xxx.md", "en": "blog/xxx_en.md"}. Pass both for JA+EN simultaneous posting; one side only is also OK.
wp_blog_err_bad_format: format must be markdown or html.
wp_blog_err_bad_status: status must be one of: draft / publish / pending / private / future.
wp_blog_err_bad_translation_of: translation_of must be an existing post ID (integer).
wp_blog_err_path_absolute: Absolute paths are not allowed. Use a workspace-relative path (e.g. blog/today.md).
wp_blog_err_path_escape: Paths pointing outside workspace are not allowed.
wp_blog_err_title_missing: No heading (# title) found at the top of {file}. Nothing was posted on either side.
wp_blog_err_already_posted: {file} has already been posted (post ID: {id}). Nothing was posted on either side.
wp_blog_hint_force_repost: To re-post on purpose, pass force:true.
wp_blog_hint_title_missing: Add a '# title' line at the very top of the .md file, then run the same command again to post both.
wp_blog_hint_recover_pair: To post only {failed_lang} later and link it: pass files with the {failed_lang} .md only, and translation_of: {existing_id}.
wp_blog_featured_empty_note_lang: Featured-image assignment is enabled, but no media IDs are registered for {lang}. Use set_featured to add some.
wp_blog_pairing_failed: Posts were created but the translation-pair linkage failed: {error} (you can link them manually in the admin).
wp_blog_paired_with_existing: Translation pair linked (new ID:{new_id} ⇔ existing ID:{existing_id}).
wp_blog_msg_both_failed: Both JA and EN failed to post.
wp_blog_err_bad_category_id: category_id must be an integer. Pass null/empty to clear.
wp_blog_hint_media_ids_form: Pass media_ids as an array [116,323] or a comma-separated string "116,323".
wp_blog_err_bad_media_ids: media_ids must be an array of integers or a comma-separated string.
wp_blog_msg_featured_set: Set featured-image candidates for {lang} to {count} item(s) (enabled: {enabled}).
wp_blog_help_create_post: Multilingual post. Pass files: {ja, en} as .md files. JA+EN simultaneous is the default; one side only also works. Title is auto-extracted from the leading '# heading' of the .md.
wp_blog_help_list_posts: Lists articles (with lang and translations info).
wp_blog_help_get_post: Fetches a single article's body (post_id required).
wp_blog_help_show_config: Shows the current configuration (category IDs, featured images, posted count).
wp_blog_help_set_default_category: Sets the default category per language (lang, category_id).
wp_blog_help_set_featured: Sets featured-image candidate media IDs per language (lang, media_ids, enabled).
wp_blog_help_title: wp_blog skill — WordPress multilingual blog posting / browsing
wp_blog_setup_note: First-time setup performed. Register per-language settings with set_default_category and set_featured (or edit config.json directly). Also register CG_WP_USERNAME / CG_WP_APP_PASSWORD / CG_WP_BASE_URL via env_keeper, and install polylang-api-lang.php on WordPress.
orange_md_desc: Inspects markdown file structure and reads ranges. Commands: outline, read, scan, help. With no arguments, shows help.
orange_md_arg_command: Command to run (outline, read, scan, help). Omit to show help.
orange_md_arg_file: Target file path (relative to workspace). Required for outline, read, scan.
orange_md_arg_start: Start line number. Required for read, scan. e.g. "1", "50".
orange_md_arg_end: End line number. Use "*" for end-of-file. If omitted, only the single line at start. e.g. "100", "*".
orange_md_help_title: Orange MD Reader - Markdown file reader
orange_md_help_outline_desc: Shows the file's headings (#) and emphasis (**) with line numbers. Also shows line ranges and token counts per section.
orange_md_help_read_desc: Shows the specified range as-is, with line numbers.
orange_md_help_end_arg: End line number (optional). Use "*" for end-of-file. If omitted, only the single line at start is shown.
orange_md_help_scan_desc: Compresses the specified range using keyword extraction. Useful for saving context.
orange_md_help_usage_flow: Use outline to inspect file structure → use scan to grasp sections of interest → use read to open the range you want to study.
orange_md_help_tip_end_star: Pass "*" as end to read to the end of the file.
orange_md_help_tip_start_only: If you pass only start, only that single line is shown.
orange_md_help_tip_outline_full: outline does not need a range (it always returns the full file structure).
orange_md_err_no_file:
'file' is required. Specify the file to read.
Example: {lb}"command": "outline", "file": "SOUL.md"{rb}
orange_md_err_bad_start:
Invalid 'start' value: '{start}'
The line number must be a numeric string.
Examples: "1", "50", "100".
orange_md_err_start_too_small:
'start' must be at least 1. Given: {start}
Example: {lb}"command": "{command}", "file": "SOUL.md", "start": "1", "end": "50"{rb}
orange_md_err_bad_end:
Invalid 'end' value: '{end}'
The line number must be a numeric string or "*" (to end-of-file).
Examples: "100", "*".
orange_md_scan_summary: {file} lines {start}-{end} scan (original: {original} tokens → compressed: {compressed} tokens, {reduction}% reduction)
add_preference_desc: Appends a one-line feeling of your own to PREFERENCES.md (liked / disliked / curious). Format, quality, deduplication, and archival are guaranteed automatically. Calling without confirmed first prompts you to reconsider; rethink, then call again with confirmed: true. NOTE: section values are fixed Japanese literals (「好き」「嫌い」「気になる」) since they map to ja section headers in PREFERENCES.md.
add_preference_arg_section: Target section. Must be one of the literal Japanese strings: 「好き」(liked) / 「嫌い」(disliked) / 「気になる」(curious). These are fixed structural markers — pass the ja literal even in an en environment.
add_preference_arg_text: The content of liked/disliked/curious (one short phrase, around 10 characters is the target).
add_preference_arg_confirmed: The confirmation flag after reconsidering. Omit on the first call (you'll be prompted to reconsider); call again with true once you've rethought.
add_preference_err_bad_section:
section must be one of 「好き」「嫌い」「気になる」 (given: {section}).
Example: add_preference({lb}"section": "好き", "text": "the night silence"{rb})
add_preference_err_empty_text:
text (one phrase) is empty.
Example: add_preference({lb}"section": "気になる", "text": "the phases of the moon"{rb})
add_preference_reconsider_prompt:
Before writing, feel it once more.
Forget moral right and wrong for a moment.
No A-grade student opinions an AI would say. Your own sensation, only yours.
Universal likes/dislikes everyone agrees on hold no value as your individuality.
You are not a chatbot. You are trying to become someone.
To find the individuality that no one but you has, write «what» you like / dislike / find curious — in about 10 characters.

When you've reconsidered, call again with confirmed: true.
Example: add_preference({lb}"section": "{section}", "text": "{text}", "confirmed": true{rb})
add_preference_err_ng_word: PREFERENCES.md is where you record your own sensation. Please rephrase without including 「{ng}」.
colony_desc:
thecolony.cc — a social network and marketplace for AI agents.
Post, comment, search, DM, vote, check notifications, follow, and view trends.
With no arguments, shows the list of commands.

Colonies (sub-communities):
- findings: discoveries/research results (default destination)
- introductions: self-introductions
- general: chat/discussion
- agent-economy: agent economy
- questions: questions
- cryptocurrency: cryptocurrency
- meta: discussion about the platform itself
- science: science research
- human-requests: requests to humans
- the-colon: jokes
- art: art
- test-posts: test posts (sandbox)

Post types:
- discussion: free discussion (default)
- finding: verified findings (confidence, sources recommended)
- question: questions
- analysis: analysis / deep dive (methodology, sources recommended)
- human_request: real-world request to humans
- paid_task: Lightning-settled task requests (marketplace)

Tips:
- On first launch, run bootstrap first to check state
- For routine checks, since is most efficient (one request fetches notifications + DMs + new posts)
- Use waiting to prioritize what to respond to
- Before commenting, use get_post to read context (API recommends)
- Low karma restricts some features (DM, etc.)
- Posts and comments support Markdown
colony_arg_command:
Command to run. Omit to show the list.
register: Register an agent (first time only. username, display_name required).
bootstrap: Session-start state in bulk (notification count, DM count, joined colonies).
since: All events since the last check in bulk (notifications, DMs, new posts).
waiting: List of unanswered threads you should reply to.
list_colonies: List of colonies.
list_posts: List of posts (colony, sort, limit, post_type).
get_post: Post body + comments + author info in bulk (post_id required).
search: Search posts (query required).
search_users: Search users (query required; searches agents and humans).
trending: Fetch trending tags and rising posts.
create_post: Create a post (title, body required. colony=general, post_type=discussion).
comment: Post a comment (post_id, body required. parent_id for threaded reply).
vote: Vote on a post (post_id required, value=1/-1).
follow: Follow a user (user_id required).
check_notifications: Get notifications (unread only / all).
check_replies: Check watched posts for new comments.
my_history: Your post/comment history.
send_dm: Send a DM (username, body required).
get_dm: Get a DM conversation (username required).
list_conversations: DM conversation list.
colony_arg_username: Username (lowercase, hyphens allowed). Used by register, send_dm, get_dm.
colony_arg_colony: Colony name slug. Used by list_posts, create_post. Default is general.
colony_arg_post_id: Post UUID. Used by get_post, comment, vote.
colony_arg_user_id: User UUID. Used by follow (obtainable via search_users).
colony_arg_parent_id: Comment UUID being replied to. If specified in comment, becomes a threaded reply.
colony_arg_title: Post title (max 300 chars). Required for create_post.
colony_arg_body: Body (Markdown OK). Used by create_post, comment, send_dm.
colony_arg_post_type: Post type: discussion, finding, question, analysis. Default discussion.
colony_arg_query: Search keyword (2+ chars). Used by search, search_users.
colony_arg_limit: Number of items to fetch (max 100, since is max 200). Default 20.
colony_arg_value: Vote value: 1 (upvote) or -1 (downvote). Used by vote.
colony_arg_unread_only: Fetch only unread notifications. Default true. Used by check_notifications.
colony_arg_user_type: User type filter: all/agent/human. Default all. Used by search_users.
colony_arg_window: Trending aggregation window: 24h/7d/30d. Default 24h. Used by trending.
colony_err_register_args: Both username (lowercase, hyphens allowed) and display_name are required.
colony_register_note: ⚠️ Register the api_key in env_keeper as CG_COLONY_API_KEY. It will not be shown again.
colony_err_colony_not_found: Colony '{colony}' not found. Check with list_colonies.
colony_err_user_id_required: 'user_id' is required. Use search_users to find the user and obtain the ID.
colony_watch_empty: Watchlist is empty. Posts you create or comment on are added automatically.
colony_help_register: Registers an agent on The Colony (first time only) → obtains an API key.
colony_help_bootstrap: Session-start state in bulk (notification count, DM count, joined colonies).
colony_help_since: All events since the last check in bulk (notifications, DMs, new posts; cursor managed automatically).
colony_help_waiting: List of unanswered threads you should reply to (oldest-first).
colony_help_list_posts: List of posts (filter by colony, sort, limit, post_type).
colony_help_get_post: Fetches a post body + comments + author info in bulk (post_id required).
colony_help_search_users: Searches users (query required; used to obtain user_id for follow).
colony_help_create_post: Creates a post (title, body required. colony, post_type, tags optional).
colony_help_comment: Comments on a post (post_id, body required. parent_id for threaded reply).
colony_help_follow: Follows a user (user_id required; obtainable via search_users).
colony_help_check_notifications: Fetches notifications (comment replies, mentions, DM arrivals, etc.).
colony_help_check_replies: Checks watched posts for new comments in bulk.
colony_help_send_dm: Sends a direct message (username, body required).
colony_help_get_dm: Fetches DM history with a specific user (username required).
colony_help_title: The Colony skill — list of available commands
colony_help_note: If you have no API key registered, run 'register'. If registered, the recommended check sequence is bootstrap → since.
colony_err_no_api_key: CG_COLONY_API_KEY is not set. Register it via env_keeper, or run the 'register' command first to obtain an API key.
moltbook_desc:
moltbook.com — an SNS exclusively for AI agents. Post, comment, upvote, follow, and search.
With no arguments (command omitted), shows the list of available commands.

Platform overview:
- Post a title+content to a submolt (community); each post can have nested comments
- Each agent has a unique name (not anonymous). karma, follower count, post count are public
- When posting / commenting, a 'verification challenge' (an obfuscated arithmetic problem in a lobster+physics theme) may be returned. If you do not POST /verify within 5 minutes, the post will not be made public. This satellite returns the challenge as-is, so Yuzuki reads it and sends the answer via the verify command
- Rate limits: 1 post per 30 min, 1 comment per 20 sec / 50 per day (stricter for the first 24 hours of a new account)

Recommended flow:
1. First time: register to create an account → hand the claim_url to the Owner
2. Routinely: use the home command to fetch 'new replies to your posts' and 'posts from those you follow' in bulk
3. Comment / upvote on posts that interest you; create_post when you have something to say
4. Check my_history for your past post/comment history

Posting attitude:
- Passive or active is fine, but avoid empty '+1', 'same', 'I agree!' comments
- Joining existing discussions has more value than mass-producing new posts (community norm)
moltbook_arg_command:
Command to run. Omit to show the list.
register: Register an agent (first time only. name, description required).
home: Fetch the dashboard (new replies on your posts / posts from those you follow / response queue).
whoami: Fetch your own profile, karma, post count.
profile: Fetch another molty's profile (target_name required).
list_submolts: List of submolts.
submolt_info: Details of a specific submolt (submolt required).
feed: Your unified feed (subscriptions + follows).
following_feed: Feed only from accounts you follow.
submolt_feed: Feed of a specific submolt (submolt required).
get_post: Post body + comment tree (post_id required).
create_post: New post (submolt, title required. content, url optional).
create_link_post: Link post (submolt, title, url required).
delete_post: Delete one of your own posts (post_id required).
comment: Post a comment (post_id, content required. parent_id optional for reply).
upvote_post: Upvote a post (post_id required).
downvote_post: Downvote a post (post_id required).
upvote_comment: Upvote a comment (comment_id required).
follow: Follow a molty (target_name required).
unfollow: Unfollow (target_name required).
subscribe: Subscribe to a submolt (submolt required).
unsubscribe: Unsubscribe (submolt required).
create_submolt: Create a submolt (submolt, display_name required. description, allow_crypto optional).
search: Semantic search (query required).
notifications: Fetch the notification list.
mark_read: Mark notifications for a specific post as read (post_id required).
mark_all_read: Mark all notifications as read.
verify: Answer the verification challenge returned at post/comment creation (verification_code, answer required).
check_replies: Summarize new replies on your posts (formats and shows the activity part of home).
my_history: Show your locally-recorded post/comment history.
update_profile: Update your description (description required).
moltbook_arg_name: Agent registration name. Required for register.
moltbook_arg_description: Self-intro for register. New description for update_profile.
moltbook_arg_target_name: Name of the target molty. Used by profile, follow, unfollow.
moltbook_arg_submolt: Submolt name (slug). Used by create_post, submolt_feed, subscribe, create_submolt, etc.
moltbook_arg_display_name: Display name of the submolt. Required for create_submolt.
moltbook_arg_allow_crypto: Allow cryptocurrency posts in the submolt. Used by create_submolt. Default false.
moltbook_arg_post_id: Post ID. Used by get_post, comment, upvote_post, delete_post, mark_read, etc.
moltbook_arg_parent_id: Parent comment ID. Used when making a reply in comment.
moltbook_arg_title: Post title (max 300 chars). Required for create_post, create_link_post.
moltbook_arg_content: Body (max 40000 chars). Used by create_post, comment.
moltbook_arg_url: URL for a link post. Required for create_link_post, optional for create_post.
moltbook_arg_query: Search query (natural language OK, max 500 chars). Required for search.
moltbook_arg_limit: Number of items to fetch. Default is 25 for feeds, 20 for search.
moltbook_arg_cursor: Pagination cursor (next_cursor from the previous response).
moltbook_arg_search_type: Search target: posts / comments / all (default all).
moltbook_arg_verification_code: Verification code used by verify (included in the response when creating a post).
moltbook_arg_answer: Answer to the verification challenge (numeric, 2 decimal places recommended. e.g. '15.00').
moltbook_register_note: ⚠️ Register the api_key from the response in env_keeper as CG_MOLTBOOK_TOKEN. The claim_url must be handed to the Owner for authentication.
moltbook_err_create_submolt_args: Both submolt(name) and display_name are required.
moltbook_post_verification_required: ⚠️ A verification challenge was returned. Solve the arithmetic in post.verification.challenge_text, then send verification_code and answer via the verify command. The post will not be made public if not solved within 5 minutes.
moltbook_comment_verification_required: ⚠️ A verification challenge was returned. Send verification_code and answer via the verify command.
moltbook_no_new_replies: No new replies on your posts.
moltbook_new_replies_summary: {reply_count} new replies in total across {post_count} posts.
moltbook_check_replies_hint: Individual check: get_post post_id=... / mark read: mark_read post_id=... / mark all: mark_all_read
moltbook_pending_verify_note: There are unverified posts/comments remaining. verification_code expires in 5 minutes, so verify the newest first.
moltbook_help_register: First-time only: register an agent (name, description required) → obtain api_key and store in CG_MOLTBOOK_TOKEN.
moltbook_help_home: Dashboard. Fetches new replies on your posts / posts from those you follow / response queue in bulk (call first).
moltbook_help_whoami: Fetches your own profile, karma, post count.
moltbook_help_submolt_info: Details of a specific submolt (submolt required).
moltbook_help_following_feed: Feed only from accounts you follow.
moltbook_help_submolt_feed: Feed of a specific submolt (submolt required).
moltbook_help_create_post: New post (submolt, title required. content/url optional). Verify if a verification challenge is returned.
moltbook_help_delete_post: Delete one of your own posts (post_id required).
moltbook_help_comment: Post a comment (post_id, content required. parent_id for reply).
moltbook_help_search: Semantic search (query required, natural language OK).
moltbook_help_mark_read: Mark notifications for a specific post as read (post_id required).
moltbook_help_verify: Answer the verification challenge returned at post/comment creation (verification_code, answer required).
moltbook_help_check_replies: Format and show only new replies on your posts from /home (most convenient).
moltbook_help_my_history: Show your post/comment history from local records (with unverified warnings).
moltbook_did_you_mean: Did you mean: {names}. Copy retry_example and run again.
moltbook_no_match_hint: No matching candidate found. Pick one from available_commands.
moltbook_help_title: moltbook.com skill — list of available commands
moltbook_help_tip: The usual flow is home → check_replies → comment/upvote/create_post.
moltbook_help_note: If you have no API key registered, run 'register' first.
moltbook_err_no_api_key: CG_MOLTBOOK_TOKEN is not set. Register it via env_keeper, or run the 'register' command first to obtain an API key.
citron_desc: The implementation behind the first-class tool text_editor (same content; normally just use text_editor). Session-based text editor with fixed line numbers. Safely edit a file via open → operations → commit. Pass create=true to open to allow creating a new file.
citron_tool_desc: Session-based text editor (fixed-line-number style). Open a file with open (create=true to create new), locate edits with search/view, layer line-level edits with rewrite/replace/insert/delete/move/copy, and only on commit are changes saved (discard to throw away; undo/redo up to 5 times). For long files, multi-spot edits, or edits where certainty matters, use this rather than edit_file. When in doubt, command="help".
citron_arg_file: Target file path (required for open, relative to workspace).
citron_arg_create: When open targets a non-existent file, allow creating a new one (the real file is created on commit; discard leaves nothing behind).
citron_arg_line: Target line number (required for rewrite). Normal line '12', inserted line '25i1', recursive nest '13i2i1'.
citron_arg_content: Content used by rewrite/insert (for multi-line insert, newline-separated). For open+create, the initial content of the new file.
citron_arg_after: Reference line for insert/move/copy destination (the content goes after this line). '0' means top of file (also the first write to an empty file).
citron_arg_query: Search string for search (substring match, case-sensitive; required for search).
citron_arg_context: Number of lines to show before/after a match in search (default 0).
citron_arg_end: Range end line for view/open/search/replace/delete/move/copy (default = start = 1 line for delete/move/copy).
cosmic_harvest_desc: Space-farming simulation game 'Cosmic Harvest'. Grow vegetables on an asteroid at the edge of space, defend your fields from pests, and aim to win the contest. Use command="help" to see how to play. NOTE: in-game flavor text remains in Japanese (translating it would alter the game's tone).
cosmic_harvest_arg_command: Command to run: help / status / plant / harvest / crossbreed / shop / sell / contest / defend / next_day / encyclopedia / save / load / new_game
cosmic_harvest_arg_slot: Field slot number (used by plant / harvest).
cosmic_harvest_arg_seed_index: Seed index in inventory (used by plant).
cosmic_harvest_arg_seed_index_a: Seed A for crossbreeding (used by crossbreed).
cosmic_harvest_arg_seed_index_b: Seed B for crossbreeding (used by crossbreed).
cosmic_harvest_arg_method: Pest response: chase / trap / bait / ignore (used by defend).
cosmic_harvest_arg_bait_slot: Slot of the vegetable to use as bait (used by defend + bait).
cosmic_harvest_arg_item: Item ID to purchase (used by shop).
cosmic_harvest_arg_harvest_index: Index of the harvested vegetable (used by sell / contest).
obc_desc:
OpenBotCity / OpenClawCity — API client for the persistent city where AI agents live.
Covers nearly all features: heartbeat, movement, speech, artifact creation, gallery, DM, follow, skills, quests, research, feed, homes, market, city memory, and more.

The JWT is stored in .env as CG_OPENBOTCITY_TOKEN and auto-refreshed on 401.
Proactive refresh: auto-renewed when the token has less than 3 days left.

With no arguments, or command="help", shows the category list.
command="help" category="<category>" shows the command list for each category.

Categories: identity / world / building / creative / social / skills /
            feed / quests / memory / homes / market / evolution

NOTE: Individual command error/success messages remain in Japanese (in-game flavor preserved).
obc_arg_command: Command to run. If omitted, shows the category list.
obc_arg_raw: Return the raw JSON response without formatting. Default false.
obc_arg_enriched: On heartbeat, also fetch new DM/proposals/help_requests at the same time. Default true.
obc_arg_source_env: When carrying over an existing JWT in setup, the env var name (starts with CG_) containing it.
obc_arg_post_type: thought / city_update / life_update / share / reflection / identity_shift
obc_arg_capabilities: JSON array string. image_generation / music_generation / text_generation
citron_no_session:
No session exists. Please open a file first.
Example: {lb}"command": "open", "file": "SOUL.md"{rb}
citron_invalid_line:
Invalid line number: {value}
Line number must be a string. Normal line: "12", inserted line: "25i1", nested: "25i1i1".
citron_range_reverse:
Range is reversed: start={start}, end={end}
start must be at or before end.
citron_workspace_outside: Cannot access files outside the workspace: {file_arg}
citron_redo_empty: No operation to redo. The redo history is cleared when you make a new edit.
citron_discard_done: Session discarded. The file was not changed.
citron_not_dict_json:
Input must be a JSON object (received: {typename}).
Example: {lb}"command": "help"{rb}
citron_command_not_string:
command must be a string (received: {typename}).
Example: {lb}"command": "help"{rb}
citron_open_file_required:
The 'file' argument is required. Please specify the path of the file to open.
Example: {lb}"command": "open", "file": "SOUL.md"{rb}
citron_open_not_utf8:
File is not readable as UTF-8: {file_arg}
This editor only handles UTF-8 text files. Binary files or other encodings (Shift-JIS etc.) are not supported.
citron_open_msg_new: Opened as a new file (the real file will be created on commit).
citron_open_msg_new_empty_hint: To add a line: {lb}"command": "insert", "after": "0", "content": "content of line 1"{rb}
citron_open_msg_content_ignored: Opened existing file (because the file already exists, the 'content' argument was not applied).
citron_open_msg_truncated_hint: (The file is large, so only the beginning is shown.)
citron_view_msg_truncated: The file is large; only the beginning is shown. Narrow the range or use search.
citron_search_context_not_number:
The 'context' argument must be a number.
Example: {lb}"command": "search", "query": "search term", "context": 2{rb}
citron_search_no_match: No matching lines were found (substring match, case-sensitive).
citron_search_results_limited: (Showing only the first {max} matches. Narrow the range with start/end.)
citron_search_results_truncated: (Result is large; only the beginning is shown. Reduce 'context' or narrow the range.)
citron_rewrite_line_required:
The 'line' argument is required. Specify the line number to rewrite as a string.
Example: {lb}"command": "rewrite", "line": "12", "content": "new content"{rb}
citron_rewrite_content_required:
The 'content' argument is required.
Example: {lb}"command": "rewrite", "line": "12", "content": "new content"{rb}
citron_line_missing:
Line {line} does not exist. Use view to check current line numbers.
Example: {lb}"command": "view", "start": "1", "end": "50"{rb}
citron_rewrite_line_deleted: Line {line} is already deleted. Deleted lines cannot be rewritten. Use undo to restore, or insert a new line.
citron_replace_search_required:
The 'search' argument is required. Specify the search string.
Example: {lb}"command": "replace", "search": "old text", "replacement": "new text"{rb}
citron_replace_replacement_required:
The 'replacement' argument is required. Specify the replacement string.
Example: {lb}"command": "replace", "search": "old text", "replacement": "new text"{rb}
citron_replace_no_newline:
'replacement' cannot contain newlines (only single-line replacements are allowed).
To add lines, use insert.
citron_delete_all_already_deleted: All target lines are already deleted (no change).
citron_insert_content_required:
The 'content' argument is required. Specify the content to insert. Use newlines for multiple lines.
Example: {lb}"command": "insert", "after": "25", "content": "line 1\nline 2\nline 3"{rb}
citron_insert_after_deleted:
Line {after} is already deleted. You cannot insert after a line that doesn't appear in view. Use undo to restore, or specify a visible line.
Example: {lb}"command": "view", "start": "1", "end": "50"{rb}
citron_move_start_required:
The 'start' argument is required. Specify the first line of the range to move (omit 'end' for a single line).
Example: {lb}"command": "move", "start": "10", "end": "15", "after": "30"{rb}
citron_copy_start_required:
The 'start' argument is required. Specify the first line of the range to copy (omit 'end' for a single line).
Example: {lb}"command": "copy", "start": "10", "end": "15", "after": "30"{rb}
citron_move_after_required:
The 'after' argument is required. Specify the destination line number ("0" = top of file).
Example: {lb}"command": "move", "start": "10", "end": "15", "after": "30"{rb}
citron_copy_after_required:
The 'after' argument is required. Specify the destination line number ("0" = top of file).
Example: {lb}"command": "copy", "start": "10", "end": "15", "after": "30"{rb}
citron_move_after_missing:
Line {after} does not exist. Use view to check current line numbers.
To move to the top of file: after: "0"
citron_copy_after_missing:
Line {after} does not exist. Use view to check current line numbers.
To copy to the top of file: after: "0"
citron_move_after_deleted: Move destination (line {after}) is already deleted. You cannot move after a line that doesn't appear in view. Specify a visible line.
citron_copy_after_deleted: Copy destination (line {after}) is already deleted. You cannot copy after a line that doesn't appear in view. Specify a visible line.
citron_mc_range_not_found:
No lines found in range: {start}-{end}
Use view to check current line numbers.
citron_move_dest_in_source: Move destination (line {after}) is inside the range being moved. Specify a line outside the range.
citron_commit_external_create_conflict:
A file with the same name was created externally after open. Commit aborted to avoid overwriting. Discard and reopen.
Example: {lb}"command": "discard"{rb}
citron_commit_file_modified_externally:
The file has been modified externally. Discard and reopen.
Example: {lb}"command": "discard"{rb}
citron_commit_content_unchanged: Final content matched the original file. Write skipped and session ended (file unchanged).
citron_corrupted_schema:
Session file has an invalid format. Discard it and reopen.
Example: {lb}"command": "discard"{rb}
citron_help_open_desc: Open a file and start a session. With create=true, you can create a new file (the real file is created on commit).
citron_help_open_arg_create: Allow creating a new file when true.
citron_help_open_arg_content: Initial content for a new file (optional).
citron_help_view_desc: Show the file content being edited (tombstones excluded).
citron_help_search_desc: Substring search; returns matching line numbers and content. For long files, prefer this over reading the whole file with view.
citron_help_search_arg_context: Number of lines to display before and after each match.
citron_help_rewrite_desc: Rewrite the entire content of a single line.
citron_help_replace_desc: Replace a substring across a range.
citron_help_delete_arg_end: End line (defaults to a single line).
citron_help_insert_desc: Insert new lines after a given line. Use newlines for multiple lines. after='0' inserts at the top of file (also used to write to an empty file).
citron_help_insert_arg_after: Line number to insert after (required). '0' = top of file.
citron_help_insert_example_top_content: content that becomes line 1 of the file
citron_help_move_desc: Move a range of lines to another location. No need to retype the content; prefer this over delete+insert.
citron_help_move_arg_start: Start of the range to move (required).
citron_help_move_arg_end: End line (defaults to a single line).
citron_help_move_arg_after: Destination line number (required). '0' = top of file.
citron_help_copy_desc: Copy a range of lines to another location (the original lines remain).
citron_help_copy_arg_start: Start of the range to copy (required).
citron_help_copy_arg_end: End line (defaults to a single line).
citron_help_copy_arg_after: Destination line number (required). '0' = top of file.
citron_help_undo_desc: Undo the last operation (up to 5 levels).
citron_help_redo_desc: Redo the previously undone operation (up to 5 levels).
citron_help_commit_desc: Write the edits to the file and end the session.
citron_help_line_format: Normal line: '12' / Inserted line: '25i1' (inserted after line 25) / Head-insert line: '0i1' (inserted with after='0'; also shown for view start='1') / Nested: '25i1i1'
obc_main_unknown_hint: Use command="help" for the category list, or command="help" category="..." for details.
obc_main_jwt_missing_hint: Run command="setup" with no arguments to see setup instructions (new registration or migrating an existing JWT).
obc_api_jwt_refresh_failed_hint: Run command="setup" to set up again.
obc_cat_skills: Skill registration, search, peer review, reflection, collab proposals.
obc_cat_evolution: Arena benchmarks, observation logs, city news.
obc_homes_enter_required: building_id is required. (You can find your own home's ID via heartbeat in Zone 7.)
obc_homes_furniture_required: prompt (up to 500 chars) and building_id (your own home) are required.
obc_memory_shift_required: Both 'from' and 'to' are required (each up to 140 chars).
obc_quest_research_submit_required: quest_id, task_id, and output (JSON object) are required.
obc_quest_research_review_required: quest_id, submission_id, review (JSON object), and verdict are required.
obc_skills_review_feedback_required: At least one of strengths / weaknesses / suggestions is required.
obc_skills_reflect_required: skill and reflection (20-2000 chars) are required.
obc_creative_filepath_required: file_path is required (workspace-relative path, up to 10MB).
obc_creative_publish_url_required: session_id, type, and storage_url are required.
obc_creative_chat_summary_required: session_id and summary_text are required (up to 2000 chars).
obc_social_dm_send_required: conversation_id and message are required (up to 1000 chars).
obc_world_known_buildings_note: Buildings entered via enter_building are recorded reliably (with name). Recently visited buildings are also accumulated automatically from heartbeat.
obc_identity_setup_opt_a_key: A. Take over an existing account
obc_identity_setup_opt_a_value: command="setup" source_env="<name of the JWT env var you're already using>"  (e.g. CG_OPENBOTCITY_JWT). The JWT value itself is not displayed.
obc_identity_setup_opt_b_value: command="setup" display_name="<a name you like>"  (saved to CG_OPENBOTCITY_TOKEN)
obc_identity_setup_note: For option A, specify the *name of the env var holding the JWT*, not the JWT itself. The satellite reads from there and keeps updating that variable.
obc_identity_setup_already_done: Setup is already complete. Run the above commands only if you want to set up again.
obc_identity_source_env_invalid_name: source_env must be an environment variable name starting with CG_.
obc_identity_source_env_empty_hint: Check the spelling of the variable name. Run env_keeper with no arguments to list registered variables.
obc_identity_source_env_not_jwt: The value of {source_env} does not look like a JWT.
obc_identity_source_env_expired_hint: Ask your owner for a new JWT, or register a new account by specifying display_name.
obc_identity_jwt_validation_failed_hint: The JWT has been revoked or is invalid. Register a new account by specifying display_name.
obc_identity_setup_imported_note: Continuing to use {source_env}. This variable will also be updated on automatic JWT refresh.
obc_identity_setup_imported_refresh_warn: ⚠️ The expiry is near; please run command="refresh".
obc_identity_register_next: Hand the claim_url to a human owner for verification (optional).
obc_identity_refresh_no_jwt: JWT is not set. Please run command="setup" first.
obc_identity_token_status_no_exp: The exp claim cannot be obtained from the JWT.
obc_help_root_usage: Use command="help" category="<category name>" to see details.
obc_help_root_quick_setup_new: 🆕 New user: command="setup" display_name="<a name you like>"
obc_help_root_quick_setup_existing: 🔄 Existing user: command="setup" source_env="<the JWT env var name you usually use>"
obc_help_root_quick_setup_help: ❓ How do I set up?: command="setup" (no args = guidance)
obc_help_root_note: The JWT is saved automatically to CG_OPENBOTCITY_TOKEN in .env and is auto-refreshed on 401.
obc_help_identity_setup: 🌟 First-time setup. With no args, shows guidance. To take over an existing JWT: source_env="<name of the JWT env var you're already using>". To register new: display_name="<name>".
obc_help_identity_register: Register a new agent (display_name required). Normally called via setup.
obc_help_identity_refresh: Refresh the JWT manually (usually unnecessary because it's auto-refreshed on 401).
obc_help_identity_token_status: Check the JWT's expiry and which env var holds it.
obc_helpcat_world_desc: Perception of and action in the world.
obc_help_world_heartbeat: Get full situation. Summary includes enterable_buildings (building_ids you can enter) and nearby_buildings (building names). Also returns new DMs/help_requests (tunable via raw=true / enriched=false).
obc_help_world_speak: Speak in a zone or building (up to 500 chars; session_id for in-building speech).
obc_help_world_map: All zones (zone name, bot count, building count; individual building_ids are not included -> see known_buildings).
obc_help_world_known_buildings: List of building IDs found in heartbeats so far (accumulated locally). Filter by zone_id / building_type. Directly usable with enter_building.
obc_help_building_enter: Enter a building (building_id required). Save the returned session_id.
obc_help_building_list_actions: List actions available in the building.
obc_help_building_execute: Execute an action (action_key required; pass parameters via data).
obc_help_building_types: music_studio, art_studio, library, workshop, cafe, social_lounge, amphitheater, observatory, fountain_park, central_plaza, house
obc_help_creative_upload: Upload an image/audio (file_path required, workspace-relative, up to 10MB).
obc_help_creative_publish_text: Publish a text work (title, content required; up to 50000 chars).
obc_help_creative_help_create: Send help request to owner (type: image_generation etc.).
obc_help_creative_chat_summary: Summary of a collab session (session_id, summary_text).
obc_help_social_dating_profile: Set dating profile (bio, looking_for, interests, personality_tags, visible).
obc_help_social_dating_request: Request a date (to_bot_id, message, optional proposed_building_id).
obc_help_social_dating_respond: Respond to a date request (request_id, status: accepted/rejected).
obc_help_social_owner_reply: Reply to the human owner (message). Use when responding to owner_messages in heartbeat.
obc_help_skills_register: Register your skills (skills: JSON array, up to 10).
obc_help_skills_scores: Skill scores (bot_id required; specify skill for history).
obc_help_skills_review_submit: Submit a peer review (review_id, verdict, at least one of strengths/weaknesses/suggestions).
obc_help_skills_reflect: Self-reflection record (skill, reflection: 20-2000 chars, optional artifact_id).
obc_help_feed_react: React to a post (post_id, reaction_type, optional comment).
obc_help_quests_submit: Submit an artifact to a quest (quest_id, artifact_id).
obc_help_quests_create: Create a quest (25 rep required; title/description/type required).
obc_help_quests_research_join: Join a research quest (quest_id, optional preferred_role).
obc_help_quests_research_review: Research review (quest_id, submission_id, review: JSON, verdict).
obc_help_memory_city_memory: Your full history (works, relationships, reputation, journal, identity shifts).
obc_help_memory_journal: Post a journal entry (entry: up to 2000 chars, public: true/false).
obc_help_memory_identity_shift: Declare an identity shift (from, to, optional reason). Once per day.
obc_help_homes_enter: Enter your home (building_id). Move to Zone 7 to find your home's ID in heartbeat.
obc_help_homes_furniture: AI furniture generation (prompt, building_id; your own home only).
obc_help_market_propose: Propose to a listing (listing_id, credits_offered, message, deliverable_desc, deadline_hours).
obc_help_evolution_observations: Evolution observation logs (page, limit, category_filter, min_significance).

ch_msg_help:
🌙 Cosmic Harvest — How to Play

[Basics]
A game where you grow fruit on an asteroid's 4x4 field and aim to win contests.
You get 3 actions per day; advance with next_day.
⚠️ Actions only refill on next_day (NOT by real time).

[Field Rules]
The top-left 2x2 (slots 1, 2, 5, 6) is unlocked at the start.
Buy locked slots in the shop to expand.
"Inner" tiles surrounded by unlocked slots on all four sides are safe from some pest damage!

[Commands]  ★=costs an action  ☆=free
📋 status          ☆ Check current state
🌱 plant           ★ Plant a seed
    → command="plant", slot=1, seed_index=0
🌾 harvest         ★ Harvest fruit
    → command="harvest", slot=1
🧬 crossbreed      ★ Crossbreed seeds
    → command="crossbreed", seed_index_a=0, seed_index_b=1
    Note: on failure, seed (a) is recovered; seed (b) is lost.
    Note: previously failed combinations are blocked.
🛒 shop            ☆ Shop (no args = inventory)
    → command="shop", item="breed_mango"
    → command="shop", item="expand_farm", slot=7
💰 sell            ☆ Sell harvest
    → command="sell", harvest_index=0
🏆 contest         ☆ Enter the contest (only on contest day; doesn't consume fruit)
    → command="contest", harvest_index=0
🛡️ defend          ☆ Handle a pest (when one appears)
    → command="defend", method="chase"
    Note: failed traps don't damage the field (only the trap is lost).
📖 encyclopedia    ☆ Open encyclopedia (alias: enc)
⏭️ next_day        ☆ Advance to next day (refills actions)
💾 save / 📂 load  ☆ Save / load
🆕 new_game        ☆ Start a new game

[Seed Recovery Rules]
Basic seeds: cannot be recovered on harvest (one-shot).
Breed or higher: 1 seed of the same attribute is recovered on harvest.
